games.tag: Luka 60 pts · Most ever allowed by Heat

nba.note: Lakers 8-game win streak · LeBron ties Robert Parrish for most games in NBA history

nhl.note: 11-game Thursday slate — Panthers shut out Oilers in Cup Final rematch; Ovechkin 1 combined goal from 1,000

wbc.label: MLB — Spring Training
wbc.note: Venezuela WBC champions return to spring camps · Opening Night: March 25

topStories.headline: 60 points. LeBron's record night. Lakers are on fire.
body: Luka Dončić scored 60 points — a season high, and the most points ever allowed in Miami Heat history — as the Los Angeles Lakers rallied past Miami 134–126 on Thursday. Dončić was practically unstoppable in the second half, scoring 39 of his 60 after the break. He went 18-of-30 from the field, 9-of-17 from three, and 15-of-19 from the line, topping James Harden's previous Heat-opponent record of 58. In doing so he also passed D'Angelo Russell for the Lakers' single-season three-point record.

That wasn't even the only history made. LeBron James, 41 years old, posted a 19-point, 15-rebound, 10-assist triple-double and in the process tied Robert Parrish for the most games played in NBA history. The Lakers are on an 8-game winning streak and surging up the West standings. Bam Adebayo (28 pts, 10 reb) kept Miami in it, but Luka simply had no ceiling Thursday night.

topStories.headline: Wembanyama's fadeaway ends a 6-year wait — Spurs are back in the playoffs
body: Victor Wembanyama buried a 17-foot fadeaway over Oso Ighodaro with 1.1 seconds remaining to lift San Antonio past Phoenix 101–100, clinching the Spurs' first playoff berth since 2019. The 22-year-old finished with 34 points, 12 rebounds, and 3 steals. The Spurs had not made the postseason in six seasons — the longest drought in franchise history — and they ended it with one of the most dramatic shots of the year. Wembanyama is the runaway frontrunner for the Western Conference's most exciting player this season.

topStories.headline: Bobrovsky blanks the Oilers — Panthers still own Edmonton
body: Sergei Bobrovsky stopped all 21 shots he faced as the Florida Panthers shut out the Edmonton Oilers 4–0 in a Stanley Cup Final rematch Thursday night. It was Bobrovsky's 4th shutout of the season and 53rd of his career. Carter Verhaeghe and Anton Lundell scored for Florida. The Panthers have now thoroughly dominated the Oilers in their last two meetings — a statement from a team that considers themselves the gold standard of the modern NHL.

stories.headline: Lakers surge, Spurs clinch — the West is getting wild
body: Two of the night's best stories came from the West. The Lakers (now 8-game winners) are climbing the standings fast behind the Dončić-LeBron pairing operating at its absolute peak. The Spurs (43–27) have quietly put together one of the best second halves in the league and are now locked in for the postseason with Wembanyama looking better every week. Meanwhile, OKC remains the 1-seed — but the West from 3 through 8 is brutally tight with less than three weeks to go.

stories.headline: LeBron ties Parrish — and he's still producing at an elite level
body: LeBron James has now played as many NBA games as Robert Parrish — more than anyone in history. What makes Thursday different from other record nights: he didn't just show up, he posted a genuine triple-double (19/15/10) in a meaningful late-season win. At 41, he's still a first-team candidate on the Lakers. The record-setter one game ahead of him: Parrish himself, at 1,611. James needs one more to be alone at the top of the all-time games list.

stories.headline: Panthers prove the Cup wasn't a fluke — Bobrovsky in Vezina territory
body: Florida shut out Edmonton 4–0 in what amounted to a statement game. The Panthers were clean, disciplined, and Bobrovsky was impenetrable. His 4th shutout of the season cements him as a legitimate Vezina Trophy candidate. Florida's defensive structure — anchored by a deep blue line and one of the league's best goaltenders — makes them an uncomfortable first-round opponent for anyone. The Oilers, missing McDavid-level production in this game, were never really in it.

stories.headline: Ovechkin is one combined goal from history — the whole league is watching
body: Alex Ovechkin stands at 999 combined regular season and playoff goals. He scored No. 922 in the regular season on Wednesday (77 career playoff goals) — and all of hockey is waiting for No. 1,000. The Capitals play again this weekend, and there's a genuine chance it comes at home. In the same game Wednesday, rookie Cole Hutson scored his first NHL goal, making it one of the more memorable nights Washington has seen in years.

stories.headline: Spring rosters taking shape — five days until Opening Night
body: With the WBC concluded and Opening Night five days away on March 25, MLB rosters are coming into focus. The Yankees, Dodgers, and Padres are integrating WBC returnees on short ramp-up timelines. Venezuela's Eugenio Suárez and Eduardo Rodriguez are back with their clubs. Team USA's Bryce Harper resumed live batting practice in Clearwater on Thursday. Every team with WBC participants is carefully monitoring workloads — no one wants a star sidelined come April.
